functions.name: array count macro
functions.description: Compute the number of elements in a real array.
functions.longDescription:
Works only when the operand is an actual array in the current scope, not a pointer parameter.

Example workflow — array length macro:
```c
#define ARRAY_LEN(array) (sizeof(array) / sizeof((array)[0]))

int values[] = {1, 2, 3};
size_t count = ARRAY_LEN(values);
```

options.label: <stdbool.h>

options.label: <stddef.h>
options.description: Core definitions such as size_t, ptrdiff_t, NULL and offsetof.

options.label: <errno.h>
options.description: Thread-local error indicator errno and symbolic error constants.

functions.description: Classic include guard for C headers.
functions.longDescription:
Classic include guard for C headers.

Example workflow — Header guard:
```c
#ifndef MY_HEADER_H
#define MY_HEADER_H


#endif /* MY_HEADER_H */
```

functions.name: __has_include feature probe
functions.signature:
#if defined(__has_include)
#  if __has_include(<threads.h>)
#    include <threads.h>
#    define HAVE_C11_THREADS 1
#  endif
#endif
functions.insertText:
#if defined(__has_include)
#  if __has_include(<threads.h>)
#    include <threads.h>
#    define HAVE_C11_THREADS 1
#  endif
#endif
functions.description: Probe for an optional header when the compiler supports __has_include.
functions.longDescription: Probe for an optional header when the compiler supports __has_include.
functions.declaration:

#  if __has_include(<threads.h>)
#    include <threads.h>

#  endif
#endif

functions.name: C23 attribute probe

#  define NODISCARD
#endif

functions.name: portable static assert
functions.signature:
#if defined(__STDC_VERSION__) && __STDC_VERSION__ >= 202311L
#  define STATIC_ASSERT(condition, message) static_assert(condition, message)
#else
#  define STATIC_ASSERT(condition, message) _Static_assert(condition, message)
#endif
functions.insertText:
#if defined(__STDC_VERSION__) && __STDC_VERSION__ >= 202311L
#  define STATIC_ASSERT(condition, message) static_assert(condition, message)
#else
#  define STATIC_ASSERT(condition, message) _Static_assert(condition, message)
#endif
functions.description: Use static_assert in C23 and _Static_assert in C11.
functions.longDescription: Use static_assert in C23 and _Static_assert in C11.
functions.declaration:
#if defined(__STDC_VERSION__) && __STDC_VERSION__ >= 202311L

functions.name: stringize and token paste
functions.signature:
#define STRINGIZE_IMPL(x) #x
#define STRINGIZE(x) STRINGIZE_IMPL(x)
#define CONCAT_IMPL(a, b) a##b
#define CONCAT(a, b) CONCAT_IMPL(a, b)
functions.insertText:
#define STRINGIZE_IMPL(x) #x
#define STRINGIZE(x) STRINGIZE_IMPL(x)
#define CONCAT_IMPL(a, b) a##b
#define CONCAT(a, b) CONCAT_IMPL(a, b)
functions.description: Use # to stringify a macro argument and ## to concatenate tokens.
functions.longDescription: Use # to stringify a macro argument and ## to concatenate tokens.
functions.declaration:
#define STRINGIZE_IMPL(x) #x
#define STRINGIZE(x) STRINGIZE_IMPL(x)
#define CONCAT_IMPL(a, b) a##b
#define CONCAT(a, b) CONCAT_IMPL(a, b)

options.label: EXIT_FAILURE

Portable success status. Prefer EXIT_SUCCESS when the intent should be explicit.
Common failure status. EXIT_FAILURE is more portable when the exact nonzero value does not matter.

printf("%s\n", TYPE_NAME(42));
functions.description: Select a string at compile time from an expression type.
functions.longDescription: Select a string at compile time from an expression type.
functions.declaration:
#define TYPE_NAME(value) _Generic((value), \
    int: "int", \
    long: "long", \
    double: "double", \
    const char *: "const char *", \
    default: "other")

printf("%s\n", TYPE_NAME(42));

functions.name: _Generic type-based dispatch
functions.description: Choose an implementation at compile time from the argument type.
functions.longDescription: _Generic is the C11 compile-time selection mechanism. It is the closest thing C has to overload resolution and is useful for typed macros, math wrappers, and API front-ends that keep a C call surface.

functions.name: X-macro + _Generic typed API front-end
functions.signature:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)
functions.insertText:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)
functions.description: Generate a family of typed entry points and one generic dispatch macro.
functions.longDescription: This pattern keeps one master type list and uses it twice: once to generate the declarations and once to build the _Generic dispatch. It scales better than hand-writing many similar wrappers.
functions.declaration:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)

functions.name: _Alignas / _Alignof
functions.description: Control and query alignment explicitly in C11.
functions.longDescription: Explicit alignment matters for SIMD, DMA buffers, lock-free structures, and ABI compatibility. _Alignas/_Alignof are the core language facilities in C11, while stdalign.h also provides the alignas/alignof convenience macros on supporting implementations.

functions.name: _Static_assert ABI contract
functions.description: Stop the build when a type layout no longer matches the binary contract.
functions.longDescription: Binary interfaces should fail fast when structure size or field offsets drift. _Static_assert is one of the most effective tools for locking down file formats, IPC packets, driver descriptors, and shared-library ABIs.

functions.name: bit fields for packed flags
functions.description: Store small integral fields inside one machine word.
functions.longDescription: Bit fields are compact, but their layout, signedness details, and packing can be implementation-defined. They are useful for local flags and documentation, but raw protocol or register layouts often still need explicit masks and shifts for predictable ABI control.

functions.name: flexible array member packet
functions.description: Attach variable-length trailing storage to a structure.
functions.longDescription: A flexible array member lets one allocation hold both the fixed header and the trailing variable-sized data. This is an important systems-programming and ABI pattern for binary messages, IPC blocks, and custom allocators.

functions.name: offsetof container field
functions.description: Query a member offset for ABI checks or intrusive structures.
functions.longDescription: Query a member offset for ABI checks or intrusive structures.

functions.name: tagged union state machine payload
functions.description: Represent one of several payload shapes with an explicit discriminator.
functions.longDescription: A tagged union is the canonical C representation for a sum type. The discriminator tells the reader which union member is currently valid. This pattern is common in protocol parsers, UI events, and device state machines.

functions.name: union for binary reinterpretation with memcpy alternative
functions.description: Overlay representations carefully and prefer memcpy for portable type punning.
functions.longDescription: Unions are often used for binary inspection, but aliasing and active-member rules are subtle across standards and compilers. When the goal is only to copy raw representation bytes, memcpy is the conservative portable technique.

functions.description: Swap the bytes of a 16-bit integer with GCC or Clang.
functions.longDescription: GCC/Clang compiler intrinsic. Use an abstraction macro when supporting several toolchains.

functions.name: __builtin_bswap32
functions.description: Swap the bytes of a 32-bit integer with GCC or Clang.
functions.longDescription: GCC/Clang compiler intrinsic. Use an abstraction macro when supporting several toolchains.

functions.name: __builtin_bswap64
functions.description: Swap the bytes of a 64-bit integer with GCC or Clang.
functions.longDescription: GCC/Clang compiler intrinsic. Use an abstraction macro when supporting several toolchains.

functions.name: container_of intrusive structure
functions.description: Recover the outer object from the address of an embedded node.
functions.longDescription: container_of is a classic intrusive-data-structure macro used heavily in kernels, runtimes, and embedded frameworks. It depends on a stable memory layout and should be paired with disciplined ownership and lifetime rules.

functions.name: double pointer out parameter
functions.description: Return allocated storage through a T ** out parameter.
functions.longDescription: A T ** out parameter is the standard C pattern when the callee must allocate or reseat a caller-owned pointer. It is also common for handle factories, DLL boundaries, and functions that grow a buffer dynamically.

functions.name: restrict-qualified pointer parameters
functions.description: Promise non-overlapping pointed ranges to enable better optimization.
functions.longDescription: restrict is a C contract saying that, for the lifetime of the restricted access, the pointed object is reached through that pointer expression family only. When the promise is true, the optimizer can vectorize and reorder more aggressively. When it is false, behavior is undefined.

functions.name: strict aliasing safe type punning with memcpy
functions.description: Read raw bytes as another scalar type without violating aliasing assumptions.
functions.longDescription: Casting one unrelated pointer type to another and dereferencing it can violate strict aliasing rules. memcpy communicates a representation copy instead of an aliasing relationship, which keeps the code portable and optimization-friendly.

functions.name: formatted I/O family guide
functions.signature: printf / fprintf / snprintf / sprintf / scanf / sscanf family guide
functions.insertText: /* Prefer snprintf for bounded string formatting. Use fprintf for FILE* streams and printf for stdout. */
functions.description: Navigation guide for formatted output and formatted input functions.

groups.description: String tokenization, bounded buffer formatting and formatted input helpers. Stream-oriented printf/fprintf cards remain under Standard Library / IO Stream.

functions.name: function table pseudo-vtable
functions.description: Bundle a set of operations into one struct of function pointers.
functions.longDescription: A function table is the C equivalent of a vtable. It is useful for hardware abstraction layers, test doubles, plugins, and protocol backends because behavior can be swapped without changing the public object layout.

functions.name: qsort-style comparator + context thunk
functions.description: Adapt a stateful comparison into a plain C callback API.
functions.longDescription: Many legacy C APIs accept a bare function pointer with no user data. A thunk or adapter layer is then needed to bridge toward a stateful comparator. On platforms that provide qsort_r or qsort_s, prefer the native context-aware variant when available.

functions.name: Win32 WNDPROC with user data in C
functions.description: Store a typed context pointer in GWLP_USERDATA and recover it in the window procedure.
functions.longDescription: Win32 often passes control through a C callback boundary. GWLP_USERDATA is the normal slot used to associate a typed application context with one window instance so the callback can recover object-like state.

functions.name: array of function pointers
functions.description: Dispatch through a small table of C function pointers.
functions.longDescription: Arrays or structs of function pointers are the standard C technique for command tables, protocol handlers, and pseudo-object interfaces.

functions.name: function returning function pointer with typedef
functions.description: Use typedef to keep function-pointer return types readable.
functions.longDescription: Raw declarations for functions returning function pointers become hard to read quickly. A typedef keeps state-machine handlers, ISR tables, and dispatch factories maintainable.

functions.name: dispatch table interface
functions.description: Represent a driver interface with function pointers and an opaque instance pointer.
functions.longDescription: Represent a driver interface with function pointers and an opaque instance pointer.

functions.name: sentinel-terminated variadic function
functions.description: Stop reading variadic arguments when a NULL sentinel is reached.
functions.longDescription: Some C APIs use a sentinel instead of an explicit count. This is flexible, but it is also fragile because the caller must remember to append the terminator with the correct type.

functions.name: va_copy reuse argument list
functions.description: Duplicate a va_list before traversing it more than once.
functions.longDescription: A va_list is a consumable cursor, not a rewindable container. If you need a second pass, copy it first with va_copy and then end both lists separately.

functions.name: va_list + va_start + va_arg + va_end
functions.description: Consume a variadic argument list with the stdarg API.
functions.longDescription: This is the core C variadic-function pattern. The fixed parameter count tells the callee how many promoted arguments to read. The extracted type passed to va_arg must exactly match what the caller supplied after the default promotions.

functions.name: variadic function declaration (...)
functions.description: Declare a C function with a fixed prefix and a variadic tail.
functions.longDescription: In C, a variadic function must declare at least one fixed parameter before the ellipsis. The caller and callee then agree on a convention such as a format string, an explicit count, or a sentinel terminator.

functions.name: variadic wrapper forwarding to vfprintf
functions.description: Forward a ... argument list to a v* stdio function.
functions.longDescription: When a function accepts ... and then delegates to printf-family formatting, the correct target is the matching v* API such as vprintf, vfprintf, vsnprintf, or vscanf. You cannot forward ... directly without first materializing a va_list.

functions.name: simple arena allocator
functions.description: Serve many short-lived allocations from one linear arena.
functions.longDescription: Serve many short-lived allocations from one linear arena.

functions.name: struct shallow copy
functions.description: Copy a structure by value in C.
functions.longDescription: A plain assignment copies each field bit-for-bit. Scalar fields are duplicated, but pointer fields still point to the same underlying memory.

functions.name: struct deep copy with heap field
functions.description: Duplicate pointed data explicitly when a C struct owns heap memory.
functions.longDescription: This is a deep copy because the destination receives its own independently allocated character buffer instead of aliasing src->name.

functions.name: dynamic vector skeleton
functions.description: Grow a simple contiguous buffer while preserving existing values.
functions.longDescription: Grow a simple contiguous buffer while preserving existing values.

functions.name: linked list traversal
functions.description: Traverse a simple singly linked list.
functions.longDescription: A classic low-level iteration pattern for intrusive structures and embedded code.

functions.name: uthash lookup note

functions.longDescription: Use uthash when a compact header-only C hash table is appropriate.

functions.longDescription: Check whether a ring buffer is empty.

functions.description: Check whether a ring buffer is full.
functions.longDescription: Check whether a ring buffer is full.

functions.longDescription: Remove one byte when available.

functions.longDescription: Read the next byte without removing it.

functions.name: DMA-style circular descriptor buffer
functions.description: Manage producer and consumer positions in a fixed hardware-oriented ring.
functions.longDescription: DMA engines, UART drivers, and data acquisition firmware often rely on circular descriptor rings. The exact ownership rules vary by device, but the pattern is always the same: fixed storage, explicit producer/consumer indices, and clear full/empty conditions.

functions.name: ARM CMSIS __DSB

functions.longDescription: ARM CMSIS intrinsic. It waits for explicit memory accesses to complete before continuing.

functions.name: ARM CMSIS __ISB
functions.description: Flush the ARM instruction pipeline through CMSIS.
functions.longDescription: ARM CMSIS intrinsic. Use only for architecture-defined cases such as control-register changes.

functions.name: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE macro
functions.insertText:
#define TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(condition) \
    do \
    { \
        if (!(condition)) \
        { \
            fprintf(stderr, "Assertion failed: %s (%s:%d)\n", #condition, __FILE__, __LINE__); \
            return false; \
        } \
    } while (0)
functions.description: Minimal boolean assertion macro for small C unit-test harnesses.

functions.name: TEST_ASSERT_EQ_INT macro
functions.insertText:
#define TEST_ASSERT_EQ_INT(expected, actual) \
    do \
    { \
        const int expectedValue = (expected); \
        const int actualValue = (actual); \
        if (expectedValue != actualValue) \
        { \
            fprintf(stderr, "Expected %d, got %d (%s:%d)\n", expectedValue, actualValue, __FILE__, __LINE__); \
            return false; \
        } \
    } while (0)
functions.description: Minimal integer-equality assertion macro.
functions.longDescription: Minimal integer-equality assertion macro.

functions.name: CMake portable C executable
functions.signature:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(my_c_app VERSION 1.0 LANGUAGES C)

set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS OFF)

add_executable(my_c_app main.c sensor.c)
target_include_directories(my_c_app PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include)

if(MSVC)
    target_compile_options(my_c_app PRIVATE /W4)
else()
    target_compile_options(my_c_app PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

functions.longDescription: A coroutine framework supplies the awaitable type and execution model.

functions.name: co_return

functions.longDescription: co_return completes a coroutine and optionally passes a result through its promise type.

functions.name: co_yield

functions.longDescription: co_yield suspends a generator while exposing one produced value.

functions.name: delete

functions.longDescription: In declarations, = delete disables an overload such as copying. Prefer RAII rather than manual delete for ownership.

functions.name: import

groups.name: Snippets
groups.description: Small reusable language skeletons that remain more useful as complete examples than as isolated calls.

functions.name: Class skeleton
functions.description: Simple class skeleton.
functions.longDescription: Baseline class pattern with constructor, accessor, mutator, and private state.

groups.name: Control flow & basic loops
groups.description: Generic branching and loop syntax. Container-specific traversal patterns remain under Iterators & Traversal.

functions.name: if statement
functions.description: Execute a block only when a condition evaluates to true.
functions.longDescription: Execute a block only when a condition evaluates to true.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Statements executed when the condition is true.

functions.name: if / else statement
functions.description: Select between two execution paths.
functions.longDescription: Select between two execution paths.

functions.name: if / else if / else chain
functions.description: Chain several mutually exclusive conditions.
functions.longDescription: Chain several mutually exclusive conditions.

functions.name: switch statement
functions.description: Branch on an integral or enumeration-like value.
functions.longDescription: Branch on an integral or enumeration-like value.

functions.name: classic for loop
functions.description: Use a classic for loop when initialization, condition, and increment must be explicit.
functions.longDescription: Use a classic for loop when initialization, condition, and increment must be explicit.

functions.name: range-based for loop
functions.description: Traverse each element of a range without managing iterators explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Traverse each element of a range without managing iterators explicitly.

functions.name: while loop
functions.description: Repeat while a condition remains true; the condition is checked before each iteration.
functions.longDescription: Repeat while a condition remains true; the condition is checked before each iteration.

functions.name: do / while loop
functions.description: Run a block at least once and test the continuation condition afterward.
functions.longDescription: Run a block at least once and test the continuation condition afterward.

functions.name: break statement
functions.description: Exit the innermost loop or switch statement immediately.
functions.longDescription: Exit the innermost loop or switch statement immediately.

functions.name: continue statement
functions.description: Skip the remainder of the current loop iteration.
functions.longDescription: Skip the remainder of the current loop iteration.

functions.name: early return guard
functions.description: Keep the main path readable by returning early when a precondition is not satisfied.
functions.longDescription: Keep the main path readable by returning early when a precondition is not satisfied.

functions.name: label declaration
functions.description: Declare a jump target inside the current function.
functions.longDescription: A label is visible throughout its containing function. Prefer structured control flow. Labels remain useful for narrow low-level cleanup or escape cases.

functions.name: goto statement
functions.description: Jump to a label inside the current function.
functions.longDescription: Use goto sparingly. It cannot jump into a scope in a way that bypasses required object initialization. Prefer loops, functions, and RAII for ordinary code.

parameters.name: labelName
parameters.description: Target label in the same function.

functions.name: goto cleanup pattern for C-style resources
functions.description: Use one cleanup label for a narrow C-style resource path when RAII wrapping is not yet available.
functions.longDescription: In idiomatic C++, prefer an RAII wrapper whose destructor releases the resource. This pattern is mainly useful at C API boundaries or during incremental refactoring.

groups.name: Error handling & exceptions
groups.description: Simple exception-handling syntax, throw patterns, catch variants, and RAII-oriented cleanup guidance.

functions.name: try / catch
functions.description: Basic exception handling.
functions.longDescription: Use for exception-aware code paths when you need to report or recover from runtime failures.

functions.name: custom exception type
functions.description: Define a small domain-specific exception.
functions.longDescription: Custom exception types can help separate categories of failures in larger codebases.

functions.name: try / catch std::exception
functions.description: Handle standard exceptions at a suitable boundary.
functions.longDescription: Catch by const reference to avoid slicing. Catch only where the program can recover, translate the error, or add useful context.

functions.name: multiple catch blocks
functions.signature: try { ... } catch (const SpecificError& ex) { ... } catch (const std::exception& ex) { ... }
functions.declaration: try { ... } catch (const SpecificError& ex) { ... } catch (const std::exception& ex) { ... }
functions.description: Handle a specific exception before a broader standard-exception fallback.
functions.longDescription: Catch blocks are tested from top to bottom. Place derived or specific exception types before broader base classes.

functions.name: catch-all handler
functions.description: Catch any exception when crossing a boundary that must not propagate failures.
functions.longDescription: A catch-all handler does not expose an exception object. Use it sparingly, typically at thread, callback, ABI, or process boundaries. Rethrow when the caller must still observe the failure.

functions.name: throw std::runtime_error
functions.description: Raise a runtime failure with a diagnostic message.
functions.longDescription: Use an appropriate standard or domain-specific exception type. Do not use exceptions for ordinary branch conditions.

functions.name: rethrow current exception
functions.description: Rethrow the currently handled exception without slicing or replacing its dynamic type.
functions.longDescription: A bare throw expression is valid only inside an active catch handler.

functions.name: noexcept function
functions.description: Declare that a function does not allow exceptions to escape.
functions.longDescription: If an exception escapes a noexcept function, std::terminate is invoked. Destructors and move operations often benefit from a correct noexcept specification.

functions.name: constructor function-try-block
functions.description: Attach a handler to construction, including failures thrown by base or member initialization.
functions.longDescription: Constructor function-try-blocks are specialized tools. They are useful for logging or translating initialization failures; the object is not fully constructed after a failed initializer.

functions.name: RAII cleanup principle
functions.description: Prefer automatic cleanup through an object destructor instead of manual cleanup paths.
functions.longDescription: RAII binds a resource lifetime to an object lifetime. It remains reliable across ordinary returns and exception unwinding.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Operations performed while the resource is owned.

groups.name: Structs, enums & type aliases
groups.description: Plain aggregates, enumerations, unions, typedefs, and modern using aliases.

functions.name: struct declaration
functions.description: Declare a plain aggregate with public members by default.
functions.longDescription: Use struct for simple value-oriented aggregates and class when encapsulation invariants dominate the interface.

functions.name: aggregate initialization
functions.description: Initialize an aggregate with brace initialization.
functions.longDescription: Brace initialization is explicit and prevents narrowing conversions in many cases.

functions.name: designated aggregate initialization (C++20)
functions.description: Initialize named aggregate members explicitly.
functions.longDescription: C++20 designated initializers improve readability for aggregates. Designators must follow declaration order in standard C++.

functions.name: struct with member defaults
functions.description: Provide safe default values directly in a structure declaration.
functions.longDescription: In-class default member initializers make default construction predictable and reduce repeated initialization boilerplate.

functions.name: unscoped enum declaration
functions.description: Declare a traditional unscoped enumeration.
functions.longDescription: Unscoped enumerators are injected into the surrounding scope and can convert to integral types. Prefer enum class for new APIs unless implicit integral behavior is required.

functions.name: enum class declaration
functions.description: Declare a scoped strongly typed enumeration.
functions.longDescription: Scoped enumerations avoid namespace pollution and accidental implicit integer conversions.

functions.name: enum class with underlying type
functions.description: Select the storage type of a scoped enumeration explicitly.
functions.longDescription: A fixed underlying type is useful for protocols, registers, serialization formats, and ABI boundaries.

options.label: std::uint8_t
options.description: Use an unsigned 8-bit underlying representation.

options.label: std::uint16_t
options.description: Use an unsigned 16-bit underlying representation.

options.label: std::uint32_t
options.description: Use an unsigned 32-bit underlying representation.

options.label: int
options.description: Use the implementation's signed int representation.

options.label: unsigned int
options.description: Use the implementation's unsigned int representation.

pickerConfig.title: Select value for enum class with underlying type.underlyingType
pickerConfig.subtitle: Underlying integral type.

sections.label: Documented choices
sections.description: Each choice includes a contextual explanation for the selected C++ API parameter.

functions.name: scoped enum value
functions.description: Refer to a scoped enumerator.
functions.longDescription: Values from enum class are qualified with the enumeration name.

functions.name: using type alias
functions.description: Create a readable modern type alias.
functions.longDescription: Prefer using aliases in modern C++. They work naturally with alias templates.

functions.name: typedef type alias
functions.description: Create a classic typedef alias.
functions.longDescription: typedef remains common in C APIs and legacy code. Prefer using aliases for new C++ code when possible.

functions.name: using alias template
functions.description: Create a parameterized alias template.
functions.longDescription: Alias templates simplify generic type composition.

functions.name: union declaration
functions.description: Declare overlapping storage representations.
functions.longDescription: Use unions carefully: only one member representation is active at a time unless a specific language rule permits inspection. For robust protocol decoding, explicit byte conversion is often clearer.

groups.name: Class lifecycle & initialization
groups.description: Class declarations, constructors, initializer lists, destructors, copy or move operations, and Rule-of-Zero guidance.

functions.name: class declaration with access sections
functions.description: Declare a class with a public interface and private state.
functions.longDescription: Classes default to private access. Keep invariants behind a small public interface.

functions.name: constructor declaration
functions.description: Declare a constructor.
functions.longDescription: Use explicit for single-argument constructors unless implicit conversion is intentional.

functions.name: constructor definition outside class
functions.description: Define a constructor outside the class body and initialize members before entering its body.
functions.longDescription: Members are initialized in declaration order, not in textual initializer-list order.

functions.name: constructor member initializer list
functions.description: Initialize bases and members efficiently before the constructor body runs.
functions.longDescription: Use initializer lists for base classes, const members, references, and values that should be constructed directly.

functions.name: in-class member initialization
functions.description: Provide a default member value in the class declaration.
functions.longDescription: In-class member initializers reduce repeated constructor boilerplate and define a clear default state.

functions.name: defaulted default constructor
functions.description: Request the compiler-generated default constructor explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Use = default when compiler-generated behavior expresses the intended semantics.

functions.name: deleted copy constructor
functions.description: Prevent copying explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Deleting copy operations is common for unique resource owners, mutex-containing objects, and handles that must not be duplicated.

functions.name: destructor declaration
functions.description: Declare a destructor.
functions.longDescription: A destructor releases owned resources. Prefer member objects and standard RAII wrappers so manual destructor code remains minimal.

functions.name: destructor definition outside class
functions.description: Define custom destruction logic outside the class body.
functions.longDescription: Destructors should not allow exceptions to escape. Prefer automatic member cleanup whenever possible.

functions.name: virtual base destructor
functions.description: Make polymorphic base destruction safe through a base pointer.
functions.longDescription: A base class intended for polymorphic deletion should generally have a virtual destructor.

functions.name: explicit single-argument constructor
functions.description: Prevent an unintended implicit conversion from one argument.
functions.longDescription: Use explicit unless an implicit conversion is deliberate and unsurprising.

functions.name: delegating constructor
functions.description: Reuse another constructor in the same class.
functions.longDescription: Delegating constructors centralize initialization logic.

functions.name: copy constructor
functions.description: Define copy construction when memberwise copy is not sufficient.
functions.longDescription: Prefer the Rule of Zero. Write copy operations only when ownership semantics require them.

functions.name: move constructor
functions.description: Transfer ownership from a temporary or expiring object.
functions.longDescription: Move constructors should usually be noexcept so standard containers can use them during reallocation.

functions.name: Rule of Zero class
functions.description: Prefer compiler-generated lifecycle operations by storing resources in RAII members.
functions.longDescription: The Rule of Zero avoids manual destructor, copy, and move code by delegating ownership to standard or dedicated wrapper types.

functions.name: inline static data member (C++17)
functions.description: Define a static data member directly inside the class declaration.
functions.longDescription: Inline static data members avoid a separate definition in one translation unit.

groups.name: Functions & overloading
groups.description: Simple declarations, definitions, overload sets, parameter passing, default arguments, and function templates.

functions.name: free function declaration and definition
functions.description: Declare a free function before defining it.
functions.longDescription: Declare a free function before defining it.

functions.name: function overloads
functions.description: Provide several functions with the same name but distinct parameter lists.
functions.longDescription: Provide several functions with the same name but distinct parameter lists.

functions.name: default function argument
functions.description: Use a default argument when callers usually use the same value.
functions.longDescription: Use a default argument when callers usually use the same value.

functions.name: nodiscard return value
functions.description: Request a warning when callers ignore an important return value.
functions.longDescription: Request a warning when callers ignore an important return value.

functions.name: function declaration
functions.description: Declare a free function before its use.
functions.longDescription: Place declarations in headers and definitions in a source file when the function belongs to a reusable component.

functions.name: function definition
functions.description: Define a free function implementation.
functions.longDescription: Keep functions focused and express outputs primarily through the return value when practical.

functions.name: overloaded free functions
functions.description: Declare several functions with the same name but distinct parameter lists.
functions.longDescription: Function overloading is selected from the argument types and qualifiers. Return type alone cannot distinguish overloads.

functions.name: default argument declaration
functions.description: Provide a default argument at the declaration site.
functions.longDescription: Specify a default argument once, typically in the header declaration. Callers may omit trailing arguments that have defaults.

functions.name: pass by value
functions.description: Pass a small value or an intentionally copied object by value.
functions.longDescription: Pass by value is natural for small scalar types and useful when the function needs its own copy.

functions.name: pass by const reference
functions.description: Read a potentially expensive object without copying it.
functions.longDescription: Use a const reference for non-owning read-only access when the function does not need to extend the object lifetime.

functions.name: pass by mutable reference
functions.description: Modify an object owned by the caller.
functions.longDescription: A non-const reference documents an output or in-out parameter. Prefer a returned value when it makes the interface clearer.

functions.name: const member function
functions.description: Declare a member function that does not mutate the observable object state.
functions.longDescription: const member functions can be called through const objects and references.

functions.name: static member function
functions.description: Declare a class-scoped function that does not receive this.
functions.longDescription: A static member function can access static members but not instance members unless it receives an object explicitly.

functions.name: simple function template
functions.signature: template<class T> ReturnType Function(const T& value);
functions.declaration: template<class T> ReturnType Function(const T& value);
functions.description: Define one generic implementation for a family of types.
functions.longDescription: Use templates when the operation is structurally identical for several compatible types.

functions.name: deleted overload
functions.description: Reject an undesired overload explicitly.
functions.longDescription: A deleted overload can produce a clear compile-time diagnostic for unsupported argument types.

functions.name: trailing return type
functions.description: Write the return type after the parameter list.
functions.longDescription: Trailing return types are useful when the result type depends on parameter names or template expressions.

groups.name: Inheritance & polymorphism
groups.description: Public inheritance, virtual dispatch, overrides, abstract bases, base-constructor forwarding, and composition guidance.

functions.name: Abstract interface
functions.description: Pure virtual interface definition.
functions.longDescription: Useful for plugin systems, test doubles, hardware abstraction layers, and dependency injection.

functions.name: Single inheritance override
functions.description: Derived class overriding a virtual API.
functions.longDescription: Classic inheritance pattern with override for polymorphic behavior.

functions.name: Protected inheritance helper
functions.description: Protected helper members in a base class.
functions.longDescription: Shows how common implementation helpers can be exposed to derived classes while staying hidden from users.

functions.name: Composition over inheritance
functions.description: Inject a dependency as a member instead of inheriting.
functions.longDescription: Composition often keeps designs simpler and more testable than deep inheritance hierarchies.

functions.name: public inheritance declaration
functions.description: Declare an is-a relationship through inheritance.
functions.longDescription: Public inheritance models substitutability: a Derived object can be used where Base is expected. Prefer composition when the relationship is not genuinely is-a.

options.label: public
options.description: Expose the inherited or declared members through public access.

options.label: protected
options.description: Expose the inherited or declared members to the class and its derived classes.

options.label: private

sections.label: Documented choices
sections.description: Each choice includes a contextual explanation for the selected C++ API parameter.

functions.name: override virtual method
functions.description: Override a virtual base-class method and request compiler verification.
functions.longDescription: Use override consistently on derived implementations. It catches signature mismatches during compilation.

functions.name: pure virtual function
functions.description: Declare an abstract operation that derived classes must implement.
functions.longDescription: A class with at least one pure virtual function is abstract and cannot be instantiated directly.

functions.name: base constructor initializer
functions.description: Initialize the base-class subobject before the derived constructor body runs.
functions.longDescription: A derived constructor uses its initializer list to select a base constructor.

functions.name: call base implementation
functions.description: Invoke a specific base-class implementation explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Calling Base::Method bypasses virtual dispatch for that call and is useful when an override extends rather than replaces base behavior.

functions.name: final derived class
functions.description: Prevent further inheritance from a class.
functions.longDescription: Use final when a class hierarchy intentionally ends at this type.

functions.name: final override method
functions.description: Prevent derived classes from overriding a virtual function further.
functions.longDescription: A final override remains virtual but closes that branch of customization.

functions.name: inherit base constructors
functions.description: Expose selected base constructors in a derived class.
functions.longDescription: Inherited constructors reduce forwarding boilerplate when base constructors are appropriate for the derived type.

functions.name: multiple inheritance declaration
functions.signature: class Derived : public Base1, public Base2 { ... };
functions.declaration: class Derived : public Base1, public Base2 { ... };
functions.description: Inherit from more than one base class when the model genuinely requires it.
functions.longDescription: Multiple inheritance is often reasonable for small interface-like bases. Use care with stateful bases and ambiguous members.

groups.name: Operator overloading
groups.description: Operator overload syntax, scope rules, member versus non-member design, friend declarations, and practical examples.

groups.name: Overview & scope rules
groups.description: Rules and decision guides for choosing member, non-member, namespace-scope, or friend overloads.

functions.name: operator overloading scope decision guide
functions.signature: member vs non-member vs friend overload
functions.declaration: member vs non-member vs friend overload
functions.insertText:
/*
Member overload:
  - left operand is *this
  - required for =, [], (), -> and conversion operators

Non-member namespace-scope overload:
  - useful for symmetric binary operators
  - allows implicit conversion on either operand

Friend non-member overload:
  - same call syntax as a namespace-scope non-member
  - additionally receives access to private/protected members
  - friend does not make it a class member

Hidden friend definition inside a class:
  - discovered by argument-dependent lookup (ADL)
  - keeps the overload associated with the class
*/
functions.description: Choose the correct declaration scope before implementing an overload.
functions.longDescription: Operator scope changes overload resolution and encapsulation. Prefer a member for operators that mutate or inherently act on the current object. Prefer a non-member for symmetric binary operations. Add friendship only when access to private state is actually needed.

functions.name: operators that must be members
functions.insertText:
/* Operators that must be non-static members:
   operator=
   operator[]
   operator()
   operator->
   conversion operators such as explicit operator bool()
*/
functions.description: Reference list of overload forms that cannot be implemented as ordinary free functions.
functions.longDescription: Reference list of overload forms that cannot be implemented as ordinary free functions.

functions.name: operator overload semantic guideline
functions.insertText:
/* Overload an operator only when its meaning remains natural.
   Keep + compatible with += when both exist.
   Keep == coherent with ordering.
   Avoid overloading &&, ||, or comma when short-circuit expectations would be misleading.
*/
functions.description: Keep overloaded operators predictable for readers and callers.
functions.longDescription: Keep overloaded operators predictable for readers and callers.

groups.name: Member overloads
groups.description: Operators naturally attached to the left-hand object, including assignment, indexing, call syntax, and increments.

functions.name: operator+ value type
functions.description: Binary arithmetic operator on a value type.
functions.longDescription: Useful for math types or other lightweight value objects where operator semantics remain intuitive. As a member overload, the left operand must already be the class type. Use a non-member or friend overload when implicit conversion should work symmetrically on both operands.

functions.name: operator[] indexing
functions.description: Subscript operator for an indexed class.
functions.longDescription: Provide both const and non-const overloads so indexed access works in read-only and writable contexts. operator[] must be a non-static member function. Provide const and non-const overloads when both read-only and writable access are required.

functions.longDescription: C++ has no super() keyword. Write Base(arguments) in the derived constructor initializer list.

functions.name: Java super.method() equivalent: Base::Method()

functions.longDescription: C++ uses scope resolution with the base-class name rather than super.method().

functions.name: Java instanceof equivalent: dynamic_cast pointer
functions.description: Check a runtime derived type through a polymorphic base pointer.
functions.longDescription: This is the closest direct equivalent of object instanceof Sensor. The base must be polymorphic, normally by declaring at least one virtual function.

functions.name: dynamic_cast reference throws std::bad_cast
functions.description: Checked downcast when a reference is required.
functions.longDescription: A failed pointer cast returns nullptr. A failed reference cast throws std::bad_cast.

functions.name: typeid exact runtime type check
functions.description: Compare the exact dynamic type.
functions.longDescription: typeid checks an exact type; dynamic_cast also matches derived classes.

functions.name: virtual dispatch instead of instanceof
functions.description: Prefer polymorphism when behavior varies by derived type.
functions.longDescription: Repeated instanceof-style branches often indicate that the operation belongs in a virtual member function.

functions.name: std::holds_alternative for std::variant
functions.description: Check an alternative in a closed type-safe union.
functions.longDescription: For a closed known type set, std::variant can be safer than RTTI.

functions.name: std::is_base_of_v compile-time relationship
functions.description: Check inheritance at compile time.
functions.longDescription: This differs from instanceof: it asks a compile-time question about types, not a runtime object.

functions.name: Java-to-C++ inheritance quick reference
functions.description: Reference map for common Java spellings.
functions.longDescription: C++ expresses the same concepts with explicit base names, virtual functions, RTTI and scope resolution.

functions.name: Container selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::array<T, N>       fixed-size contiguous storage
 std::vector<T>         resizable contiguous storage; default sequence choice
 std::deque<T>          fast insertion/removal at both ends
 std::list<T>           stable iterators; O(1) splice/erase with iterator
 std::forward_list<T>   compact singly-linked list
 std::string            owning text buffer
 std::string_view       non-owning read-only text view
 std::span<T>           non-owning contiguous range view (C++20)
*/
functions.description: Choose a sequence container according to storage, iterator stability, and mutation needs.
functions.longDescription: Use std::vector by default for general sequences. Reach for list-like containers only when their specific iterator-stability or splice properties matter.

functions.name: Ordered associative selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::set<Key>              unique sorted keys
 std::multiset<Key>         sorted keys with duplicates
 std::map<Key, Value>       unique sorted key/value pairs
 std::multimap<Key, Value>  sorted key/value pairs with duplicate keys

 Typical lookup cost: logarithmic.
 Use when sorted iteration, lower_bound, or range queries matter.
*/
functions.description: Choose ordered tree-based associative containers.
functions.longDescription: Ordered associative containers maintain key ordering and expose lower_bound, upper_bound, and equal_range.

functions.name: std::set<Key>
functions.signature: template<class Key, class Compare = std::less<Key>, class Allocator = std::allocator<Key>> class std::set

functions.name: Unordered associative selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::unordered_set<Key>              unique hashed keys
 std::unordered_multiset<Key>         hashed keys with duplicates
 std::unordered_map<Key, Value>       unique hashed key/value pairs
 std::unordered_multimap<Key, Value>  hashed key/value pairs with duplicate keys

 Average lookup cost is typically constant-time.
 Iteration order is unspecified and can change after rehash.
*/
functions.description: Choose hash-table associative containers.
functions.longDescription: Use unordered containers for key-based lookup when sorted iteration and ordered range queries are not required.

functions.name: unordered iteration and invalidation reminder
functions.insertText:
/*
 unordered_* iteration order is unspecified.
 Rehashing invalidates iterators.
 References and pointers to stored elements remain valid across rehash,
 but erasing an element invalidates references and pointers to that element.
 Do not rely on output ordering for logs or serialized files.
*/
functions.description: Document the main unordered-container pitfalls.
functions.longDescription: Use an ordered container or sort extracted keys when deterministic iteration order is required.

functions.name: Container adaptor selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::stack<T>          LIFO access: push, pop, top
 std::queue<T>          FIFO access: push, pop, front, back
 std::priority_queue<T> highest-priority access: push, pop, top

 Adaptors deliberately expose a restricted interface.
*/
functions.description: Choose a restricted queue-like interface when full container traversal is unnecessary.
functions.longDescription: Container adaptors express access policy clearly and prevent accidental arbitrary mutation.

functions.name: std::isspace
functions.description: Classify one character with std::isspace.
functions.longDescription:
The <cctype> functions accept an unsigned-char value converted to int, or EOF. Cast signed char input to unsigned char before classification to avoid undefined behavior for negative values.

```cpp
const bool digit = std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(ch)) != 0;
```

The <cctype> functions accept an unsigned-char value converted to int, or EOF. Cast signed char input to unsigned char before classification to avoid undefined behavior for negative values.

```cpp
const bool digit = std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(ch)) != 0;
```

functions.name: Iterator category quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 input iterator         read a single-pass sequence
 output iterator        write a single-pass sequence
 forward iterator       multi-pass forward traversal
 bidirectional iterator forward and backward traversal
 random-access iterator constant-time jumps and ordering
 contiguous iterator    random-access plus contiguous memory (C++20)
*/
functions.description: Understand iterator capability levels.
functions.longDescription: Algorithms declare the minimum iterator capabilities they require. For example, std::sort needs random-access iterators, while std::find only needs input iterators.

functions.name: container iterator types
functions.description: Use mutable, const, reverse, and const-reverse iterators.
functions.longDescription: Prefer auto for local iterator declarations unless the explicit type improves teaching or API clarity.

functions.name: classic indexed loop
functions.description: Traverse an indexable sequence while retaining its index.
functions.longDescription: Use an indexed loop when the numeric position is required. Prefer range-for for simple value traversal.

functions.name: classic iterator loop
functions.description: Traverse a container explicitly through iterators.
functions.longDescription: Explicit iterators are useful when passing positions to insert/erase or when controlling traversal precisely.

functions.name: const iterator loop
functions.description: Traverse without permitting element mutation.
functions.longDescription: cbegin and cend make read-only intent explicit.

functions.name: range-based for value reference const reference

functions.description: Choose copy, mutable reference, or const reference in a range-for loop.
functions.longDescription: For non-trivial elements, const T& is the usual read-only traversal form. Use T& when mutating elements.
functions.declaration: range-based for value reference const reference

functions.name: erase while iterating map

functions.longDescription: Modern associative-container erase(iterator) returns the iterator following the removed element.

functions.name: Algorithm selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 Search:       find, find_if, count_if, any_of, all_of, none_of
 Ordering:     sort, stable_sort, partial_sort, nth_element, lower_bound
 Mutation:     remove_if + erase, std::erase_if, unique, partition
 Transform:    transform, for_each, copy, move
 Aggregation:  accumulate, reduce, minmax_element
 Sets:         set_union, set_intersection, set_difference
 C++20 ranges: std::ranges::find, sort, transform, views pipelines
*/
functions.description: Prefer standard algorithms when they state the intent clearly.
functions.longDescription: Algorithms work through iterators or ranges and reduce manual loop boilerplate.

functions.name: lambda syntax quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 []                 capture nothing
 [value]            capture one variable by value
 [&value]           capture one variable by reference
 [=]                capture used automatic variables by value
 [&]                capture used automatic variables by reference
 [&, threshold]     reference default, threshold by value
 [=, &result]       value default, result by reference
 [this]             capture this pointer
 [*this]            copy current object (C++17)
 [name = expression] init-capture (C++14)
*/
functions.description: Reference the main lambda capture forms.
functions.longDescription: Capture deliberately. Avoid broad reference captures for callbacks that can outlive the current scope.

functions.name: captureless lambda
functions.description: Declare a lambda without external state.
functions.longDescription: Captureless lambdas are small function objects and can convert to compatible plain function pointers.

functions.name: capture by value and reference
functions.description: Mix value and reference captures explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Value captures copy state into the closure. Reference captures alias external state and require careful lifetime management.

functions.name: default captures and explicit exceptions
functions.description: Use a default capture while overriding one variable explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Prefer explicit captures in long-lived callbacks because they make dependencies and lifetimes easier to audit.

functions.name: capture this and copy object
functions.description: Distinguish capturing an object pointer from copying the object state.
functions.longDescription: [this] captures a pointer and can dangle. [*this] copies the current object into the closure when the type is copyable.

functions.name: init capture and move capture
functions.description: Initialize a closure field directly and move ownership into a callback.
functions.longDescription: Init-capture is useful for renamed variables, computed capture values, and move-only resources.

functions.name: mutable lambda state
functions.description: Permit mutation of values stored inside the closure object.
functions.longDescription: By-value captures are logically const inside operator() unless mutable is specified.

functions.name: Lambda capture by value/reference
functions.description: Basic lambda capture patterns.
functions.longDescription: Captures by value copy external state, while captures by reference let the lambda observe or modify external variables.

functions.name: Generic lambda
functions.description: Lambda with auto parameters.
functions.longDescription: Generic lambdas provide a concise way to write templated function objects.

functions.name: lambda parameters return type noexcept
functions.description: Specify parameters, exception contract, and trailing return type.
functions.longDescription: A trailing return type is useful when deduction is insufficient or when documenting an interface.

functions.name: generic lambda auto parameters
functions.description: Declare a templated call operator through auto parameters.
functions.longDescription: Generic lambdas are concise reusable function objects for algorithms and local helpers.

functions.name: templated lambda C++20
functions.description: Declare explicit template parameters on a lambda.
functions.longDescription: C++20 templated lambdas are useful when auto parameters alone cannot express the intended constraints or explicit template arguments.

functions.name: constexpr lambda
functions.description: Use a lambda during constant evaluation when its body permits it.
functions.longDescription: Lambdas can participate in compile-time computation and local metaprogramming helpers.

functions.name: recursive lambda self parameter
functions.description: Implement a local recursive lambda without std::function.
functions.longDescription: Passing the closure to itself avoids type-erasure overhead and keeps the helper local.

functions.name: lambda immediately invoked
functions.description: Use an immediately invoked lambda to compute a const value with structured control flow.
functions.longDescription: This pattern keeps temporary setup logic local while allowing const initialization.

functions.name: captureless lambda to function pointer
functions.description: Convert a captureless lambda to a compatible function pointer.
functions.longDescription: A lambda with captures cannot convert to a plain function pointer because it needs closure state.

functions.name: std::function callback storage
functions.description: Store heterogeneous copyable callbacks behind one signature.
functions.longDescription: std::function performs type erasure and may allocate. Use it when runtime callback replacement is useful and the overhead is acceptable.

functions.name: template callback parameter zero overhead
functions.description: Accept an arbitrary callback without std::function type erasure.
functions.longDescription: A forwarding template is suitable for hot paths and compile-time composition when ABI stability is not required.

functions.name: std::invoke function member pointer callable
functions.description: Invoke functions, function objects, member functions, or data-member pointers uniformly.
functions.longDescription: std::invoke is a useful generic dispatch primitive for callback frameworks and adapter utilities.

functions.name: std::bind and lambda preferred
functions.description: Bind arguments to a callable and compare with the clearer lambda form.
functions.longDescription: std::bind remains available, but lambdas usually make captures, argument order, and lifetimes easier to read.

functions.name: callback list event dispatch
functions.description: Store and emit a simple list of callbacks.
functions.longDescription: For production event systems, add disconnection tokens, exception policy, reentrancy rules, and thread-safety decisions.

functions.name: std::function callback
functions.description: Store a callback using std::function.
functions.longDescription: Good for callbacks with type erasure, though function pointers or templates may be cheaper in hot paths.

functions.name: avoid dangling reference capture
functions.description: Recognize the lifetime hazard of returning a reference-capturing callback.
functions.longDescription: Reference captures are safe only while the referenced objects remain alive. This is especially important for deferred, GUI, and threaded callbacks.

functions.name: weak_ptr callback guard
functions.description: Avoid extending lifetime or dereferencing a destroyed shared object from a callback.
functions.longDescription: weak_ptr is a common technique for callbacks registered into systems that may outlive the observed object.

functions.name: thread lambda value capture
functions.description: Move owned state into a worker thread callback.
functions.longDescription: Avoid capturing stack references unless the thread is joined before those references can expire and concurrent access is synchronized.

functions.name: jthread stop token callback C++20
functions.description: Use a cooperative cancellation token inside a C++20 jthread callback.
functions.longDescription: std::jthread requests stop and joins automatically at destruction, which is safer than unmanaged thread shutdown paths.

functions.name: move-only callback note C++23
functions.insertText:
/*
 C++23: std::move_only_function<void()> can store move-only callables.
 Use it when a callback owns move-only state and copying is not required.
 Availability depends on the selected standard library implementation.
*/
functions.description: Document the C++23 move-only callback wrapper.
functions.longDescription: std::function requires copyable targets. std::move_only_function addresses callbacks that intentionally own move-only resources.

functions.name: binary POD warning
functions.signature:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/
functions.description: Document why dumping arbitrary C++ objects is unsafe.
functions.longDescription: Direct memory dumps are only appropriate for tightly-controlled trivial layouts with an explicit binary contract.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/

functions.longDescription: Expose a mutable byte view over a contiguous buffer.

functions.name: binary header encode decode caution
functions.description: Avoid persisting native object layout when a format must remain portable.
functions.longDescription: For durable files and network protocols, encode each field explicitly, define byte order, validate lengths, and version the format. std::memcpy is suitable for copying bytes, not for defining a portable wire format.

functions.name: POSIX open read close file descriptor
functions.description: Open and read a file with Linux/POSIX file descriptors.
functions.longDescription: Use the POSIX file-descriptor API when integrating with Linux-specific devices, descriptors, poll/epoll, or system calls. For ordinary portable files, prefer std::ifstream and std::filesystem.

functions.name: POSIX write all bytes helper
functions.description: Write an entire byte buffer while handling partial writes and EINTR.
functions.longDescription: POSIX write may transfer fewer bytes than requested. Loop until the complete buffer has been written, and retry interrupted calls.

functions.name: Linux mmap read-only file mapping
functions.description: Map a file into memory for efficient random access on Linux.
functions.longDescription: Memory mapping is useful for large binary files and random access. The mapped region remains valid until munmap. Handle zero-length files separately in production code.

functions.name: Linux inotify watch directory
functions.description: Watch a Linux directory for file changes with inotify.
functions.longDescription: inotify is Linux-specific. It is appropriate for configuration hot-reload, file arrival monitoring, and local tooling. Integrate the descriptor with poll or epoll for non-blocking event loops.

functions.name: POSIX mkstemp temporary file
functions.description: Create a unique temporary file securely with mkstemp.
functions.longDescription: mkstemp creates and opens a unique file atomically, avoiding race conditions from manually generated temporary paths.

functions.name: Linux flock advisory file lock
functions.description: Protect a process-shared file with an advisory flock lock.
functions.longDescription: flock provides advisory locking on Linux. All cooperating processes must follow the same locking convention.

functions.name: serial and USB API selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/
functions.description: Choose an API according to the device and portability requirement.
functions.longDescription: C++ does not provide a standard serial-port or USB API. Use a cross-platform library or the operating-system API appropriate for the target.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/

functions.declaration: int libusb_control_transfer(libusb_device_handle* devHandle, uint8_t requestType, uint8_t request, uint16_t value, uint16_t index, unsigned char* data, uint16_t length, unsigned int timeoutMs);
functions.insertText: libusb_control_transfer(device, requestType, request, value, index, data, length, timeoutMs)
functions.description: Perform a synchronous USB control transfer.
functions.longDescription:
Perform a synchronous control transfer.

Workflow examples retained from the previous pack:

```cpp
unsigned char data[64]{};

const int transferred = libusb_control_transfer(
    device,
    LIBUSB_ENDPOINT_IN | LIBUSB_REQUEST_TYPE_VENDOR | LIBUSB_RECIPIENT_DEVICE,
    request,
    value,
    index,
    data,
    sizeof(data),
    1000);
```

functions.name: C++ backend frontend architecture
functions.signature:
/*
 Browser frontend (HTML / CSS / JavaScript)
        |
        | fetch() HTTP requests + JSON payloads
        v
 C++ backend REST API
        |
        +-- domain logic
        +-- serial / USB / TCP devices
        +-- configuration files

 Typical endpoints:
   GET  /api/status
   POST /api/config
   POST /api/command
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Browser frontend (HTML / CSS / JavaScript)
        |
        | fetch() HTTP requests + JSON payloads
        v
 C++ backend REST API
        |
        +-- domain logic
        +-- serial / USB / TCP devices
        +-- configuration files

 Typical endpoints:
   GET  /api/status
   POST /api/config
   POST /api/command
*/
functions.description: Outline a practical local or embedded web architecture.
functions.longDescription: A browser cannot call C++ functions directly. Expose an HTTP, WebSocket, or similar network boundary and exchange explicit payloads such as JSON.

functions.name: communication technology selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/
functions.description: Choose the transport and protocol layer deliberately.
functions.longDescription: Separate the physical transport from the application protocol. For example, Wi-Fi can carry the same HTTP or TCP protocol as wired Ethernet.
functions.declaration:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/

functions.name: C++ standard connectivity scope note
functions.signature:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/
functions.description: Document which communication layers are outside the C++ standard library.
functions.longDescription: Keeping platform-specific code behind narrow interfaces makes the rest of the application easier to test and port.
functions.declaration:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/

functions.name: Linux NetworkManager D-Bus Wi-Fi control note
functions.description: Use NetworkManager D-Bus or libnm for Linux Wi-Fi discovery and connection management.
functions.longDescription: NetworkManager exposes programmatic D-Bus interfaces for network management. For application data after association, use normal IP sockets. Use the management API only when the program must scan, select, or reconfigure Wi-Fi links.

functions.name: Linux nl80211 low-level Wi-Fi note
functions.description: Reference the Linux nl80211 interface for low-level wireless tooling.
functions.longDescription: nl80211 is lower-level than NetworkManager. It is appropriate for specialized wireless tooling but usually unnecessary for an application that only exchanges data over an established Wi-Fi connection.

functions.name: Bluetooth API selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 Bluetooth HID device:
   - HIDAPI may be sufficient

 Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM / custom sockets:
   - Windows: Winsock Bluetooth (AF_BTH)
   - Linux: BlueZ socket APIs or a higher-level library

 Bluetooth Low Energy:
   - use GATT APIs through the platform SDK or a BLE library
   - model services, characteristics, notifications, and pairing explicitly
*/

 Bluetooth HID device:

 Bluetooth Low Energy:
   - use GATT APIs through the platform SDK or a BLE library
   - model services, characteristics, notifications, and pairing explicitly
*/

functions.name: BLE GATT conceptual flow
functions.signature:
/*
 BLE GATT client flow:
   1. discover or select peripheral
   2. pair / bond if required
   3. connect
   4. discover services
   5. discover characteristics
   6. read / write characteristic values
   7. subscribe to notifications or indications
   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 BLE GATT client flow:
   1. discover or select peripheral
   2. pair / bond if required
   3. connect
   4. discover services
   5. discover characteristics
   6. read / write characteristic values
   7. subscribe to notifications or indications
   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/
functions.description: Document the BLE service and characteristic workflow.
functions.longDescription: BLE communication is structured around GATT services and characteristics rather than a generic serial byte stream.
functions.declaration:

 BLE GATT client flow:

   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/

functions.name: lock granularity and invariant reference
functions.signature:
/* Mutex design checklist

   - Protect an invariant, not an isolated variable by habit.
   - Keep critical sections short.
   - Avoid calling unknown callbacks while holding a lock.
   - Use one documented lock ordering rule when several mutexes exist.
   - Prefer RAII wrappers: lock_guard, unique_lock, scoped_lock.
   - Never hold a GUI lock or application mutex during blocking I/O unless required.
*/
functions.insertText:

   - Protect an invariant, not an isolated variable by habit.
   - Keep critical sections short.
   - Avoid calling unknown callbacks while holding a lock.
   - Use one documented lock ordering rule when several mutexes exist.
   - Prefer RAII wrappers: lock_guard, unique_lock, scoped_lock.
   - Never hold a GUI lock or application mutex during blocking I/O unless required.
*/

functions.name: memory_order quick reference
functions.signature:
/* std::memory_order quick reference

   seq_cst  : strongest default ordering; easiest to reason about
   acquire  : subsequent reads observe data published before a release
   release  : prior writes become visible to a matching acquire
   acq_rel  : acquire + release for read-modify-write operations
   relaxed  : atomicity only; no synchronization ordering

   Start with the default sequentially consistent ordering. Use weaker ordering
   only after proving the required happens-before relationships.
*/
functions.insertText:
/* std::memory_order quick reference

   seq_cst  : strongest default ordering; easiest to reason about
   acquire  : subsequent reads observe data published before a release
   release  : prior writes become visible to a matching acquire
   acq_rel  : acquire + release for read-modify-write operations
   relaxed  : atomicity only; no synchronization ordering

   Start with the default sequentially consistent ordering. Use weaker ordering
   only after proving the required happens-before relationships.
*/
functions.description: Reference for the principal atomic memory orders.
functions.longDescription: Reference for the principal atomic memory orders.
functions.declaration:
/* std::memory_order quick reference

   seq_cst  : strongest default ordering; easiest to reason about
   acquire  : subsequent reads observe data published before a release
   release  : prior writes become visible to a matching acquire
   acq_rel  : acquire + release for read-modify-write operations
   relaxed  : atomicity only; no synchronization ordering

   Start with the default sequentially consistent ordering. Use weaker ordering
   only after proving the required happens-before relationships.
*/
